description: Fitz and Simmons work together on Whitehall's hard drive, and the emotional toll of Simmons' absence is revealed. Their attempt at collaboration quickly turns into a confrontation over unresolved feelings about Simmons leaving and Fitz's recovery. The scene ends with Simmons distressed and exiting, showing the depth of their fractured friendship.

transcript.line: I still can't find anything on the weapons. Fitz? Ah, you're still looking at those backlogged files.
Yeah. That's because, um... those ... these two bits, there's something. I think the...
No, no. No. Um, no. It's... um... it's important, because... I... the... Can you stop, uh... stop looking at me?
Oh, I can't even look at you now.
No, not like that, you can't.
Like what? I'm... I'm trying not to treat you any differently than...
I know, but I am different. And I... and I'm... I'm trying not to be, but I am. And if for some reason you can't accept it...
Oh, that's not fair. Fitz, I'm only trying to help.
You left. I needed help. I needed help with the cloaking. I needed help with, um... with lots of other things, and someone to talk through, at least. You gave up on me.

You told me you were gonna go see your mum and your dad, and then you went off to... for all I... for all I know, you could... something could've... you could've been killed. And because what? Because you think I'm useless?
Of course I don't. That's not why I left.
Fitz... I'm sorry. I can't do this. Excuse me. Excuse me.
